title: Christophe B., an ordinary man accused of extraordinary violence in 'libertine' rapes
subhead: The 56-year-old is accused of forcing his partners to engage in extreme sexual acts with men he recruited online for over 20 years. He is charged with rapes with torture or acts of barbarity.

Bombing intensifies as US strikes Iran for the seventh night
subhead: Iran reported at least three dead and retaliated with attacks on several US military sites in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

title: Why Russia's hybrid warfare is forcing France to raise its voice
subhead: Between acts of sabotage and drone flyovers, the General Directorate of Internal Security (DGSI) attributed a large-scale cyberespionage campaign to Russian domestic intelligence. In response, the chargé d'affaires at the Russian embassy in Paris was summoned by the foreign minister on Friday, July 17.

title: French court sentences Freemason criminal masterminds to up to 30 years in prison
subhead: After more than three months of hearings in a case involving a murder, two attempted murders and several violent assaults, 22 defendants involved in the criminal activities linked to a Masonic lodge in the Paris region heard the verdicts of a special court of the French capital on Friday.

title: Frontex continuing mission in Greece despite serious violations of migrants' rights
subhead: How long will Frontex tolerate the growing violations of migrants' rights in Greece? A Le Monde investigation documents the many warnings issued by the Fundamental Rights Office of the European agency, which have so far failed to prompt meaningful action.

subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister, puts social justice at top of agenda
subhead: The Makerfield MP has been declared Labour leader and is set to succeed Keir Starmer on Monday. His platform remains vague and he will have to contend with tight public finances.
